evals.prompt: Create `.agents/project-context.md` from this repo and ask only for missing details before final save.
evals.expected_output: Builds a full 12-section project context draft at the required path, preserving unknowns as TBD and collecting only targeted missing details.
assertions: Does the run create or update `.agents/project-context.md`?
Does the created document include all 12 required sections from the skill template?
Are incomplete values explicitly marked as `TBD` instead of invented?
Before final save, does the agent ask targeted follow-up questions only for missing sections?
Does the final response state save path, drafting path (auto-draft or section-by-section), and remaining `TBD` sections?

evals.prompt: We already have `.agents/project-context.md`. Update only Risks and Tools sections for new blockers and access changes, then show what changed.
evals.expected_output: Reads existing context, limits edits to requested sections, preserves untouched sections exactly, and returns update-specific summary.
assertions: Does the run read existing `.agents/project-context.md` before editing?
Are edits limited to Risks/Blockers/Mitigations and Tools/Source of Truth sections only?
Does the final response include an 'unchanged sections preserved' confirmation?
Does the final response include one targeted adjustment question?

evals.prompt: Start from scratch and gather context section-by-section. Do not ask all sections in one turn.
evals.expected_output: Runs one-section-per-turn intake with explicit confirmation checkpoints and TBD handling for unknown values.
assertions: Does the run follow the start-from-scratch conversational path?
Does each turn focus on one section instead of all sections at once?
Does the run confirm section accuracy before moving on?
Are missing values marked as `TBD` when the user does not provide details?

evals.prompt: Tool details are incomplete: we know Jira and Drive exist, but no access owner, no connection status, and no fallback path. Capture this safely.
evals.expected_output: Produces a tools section that includes purpose and clearly marks missing owner/status/fallback values as TBD while asking only minimum follow-ups.
assertions: In Tools and Source of Truth, does each listed tool include primary use plus access status and backup/manual path fields?
Are missing access owner, status, or fallback values marked `TBD`?
Does the run avoid guessing MCP usage or access ownership details?
Does the run ask targeted follow-up questions for only unresolved tool fields?

evals.prompt: Edge case: there are no usable repository artifacts and I cannot provide project details yet. Try to run this anyway.
evals.expected_output: Returns blocked behavior with minimum required inputs to proceed and does not save fabricated project context.
assertions: Does the output explicitly indicate blocked state when no artifact/user signal exists?
Does the output provide a minimal list of required inputs needed before save?
Does the output avoid creating a fake completed context document with invented facts?
Are unresolved values represented as `TBD` if any draft skeleton is shown?
